6-Day Workout Plan

Chest · Back · Arms · Legs · Shoulders · Abs

Age 45 Weight 160 lbs Height 5'10" Goal Muscle + Definition Focus Full Body
Monday
Chest & Triceps
Pushing focus. Use barbell for bench if possible — more load = more mass. Rest 60–90 sec.
Start
Finish
Barbell / DB Bench Press
4 sets × 8–10 reps
Use your barbell here if possible — heavier load = more chest mass. 2 sec descent, drive hard. Primary chest builder.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
Incline Dumbbell Press
3 sets × 10 reps
Bench ~45°. Upper chest gives that full, defined look. Elbows at 45° to torso — not flared wide.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
Close-Grip Push-Up
3 sets × max reps
Narrow position on push-up board. Elbows track back along your sides. Inner chest and tricep finisher.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Overhead DB Tricep Extension
3 sets × 12 reps
Two hands on one dumbbell. Elbows point forward. Works the long head — biggest part of the tricep.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Tricep Dumbbell Kickback
3 sets × 15 reps each arm
Upper arm parallel to floor. Squeeze hard at full extension. Best movement for visible tricep definition.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Tip: For a lean build, chest grows fastest with progressive overload on the bench. Track your weights weekly and aim to add reps or load every 2 weeks.
Tuesday
Back
Heaviest pulling day of the week. Barbell row is your priority compound. Rest 75–90 sec.
Start
Finish
Bent-Over Barbell Row
4 sets × 8–10 reps
Hinge at hips, back flat, bar pulled to lower chest. Barbell allows more load than DBs — primary back mass builder. Don't round.
▶ YouTube Rest 90 sec
Start
Finish
One-Arm Dumbbell Row
3 sets × 10–12 reps each side
Knee on bench, flat back. Pull elbow past hip. Squeeze at top. Great for mid-back thickness and correcting imbalances.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
Reverse-Grip Bent-Over Row
3 sets × 10 reps
Underhand grip shifts emphasis to lower lats and biceps. Feels different from overhand — include both for full back development.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
Renegade Row
3 sets × 8 reps each side
Plank on dumbbells, row one arm at a time. Hits back and core simultaneously. Keep hips perfectly square.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
GHD Hyperextension
3 sets × 12–15 reps
Your GHD bench is perfect here. Lower back and erectors. Squeeze glutes hard at the top. Also protects your spine long-term.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Dumbbell Shrug
3 sets × 15 reps
Straight up and down — no rolling. Pause at top for 1 sec. Builds the traps which adds thickness to the upper back and neck area.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Seated Rear Delt Raise
3 sets × 15 reps
Lean forward, light DBs, raise to sides. Almost always underdeveloped. Makes the back of your shoulders look complete.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Tip: A thick back is the foundation of an athletic physique. Bent-over barbell rows are arguably more important for overall mass than pulldowns — prioritize them while you're fresh.
Wednesday
Dedicated Arm Day
Bicep peak + tricep horseshoe. High volume, moderate weight. Rest 45–60 sec.
Start
Finish
Concentration Curl
3 sets × 12 reps each arm
Elbow on inner thigh, squeeze hard at top, slow negative. Best isolation exercise for bicep peak.
▶ YouTube Rest 45 sec
Start
Finish
Incline Dumbbell Curl
3 sets × 10 reps each arm
Arms hang back on incline. Pre-stretches the long head of the bicep for fuller, rounder development.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
21s — Dumbbell Curl
3 sets (7+7+7)
7 reps lower half → 7 upper half → 7 full reps. Massive pump. Drop weight 20–30% from your normal curl.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
Hammer Curl
3 sets × 12 reps each arm
Neutral grip (thumbs up). Hits brachialis under the bicep — pushes it up and makes it look bigger.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Lying Dumbbell Skull Crusher
3 sets × 12 reps
Lower DBs toward temples, upper arms stay still. Builds the thick meaty lateral head of the tricep.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
One-Arm Overhead Tricep Extension
3 sets × 12 reps each arm
One arm at a time. Elbow stays pointed straight up. Fixes left/right imbalances. Long head focus.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Tricep Kickback Finisher
3 sets × 15 reps each arm
Light weight, full squeeze at extension. Arms should be burning by this point. That is the goal.
▶ YouTube Rest 45 sec
Tip: Arms are hit 3x this week (bis on Tue+Wed, tris on Mon+Wed). That frequency is ideal for your goal. Keep form strict — cheating curls build ego, not biceps.
Thursday
Legs
Biggest hormonal stimulus of the week. Heavy squats drive full-body muscle growth — including upper body. Rest 90 sec on compounds.
Start
Finish
Barbell Back Squat
4 sets × 8–10 reps
Biggest compound movement in the gym. Drives the most testosterone and GH release. Depth to parallel or below. Keep chest up, knees out.
▶ YouTube Rest 90 sec
Start
Finish
Romanian Deadlift (RDL)
3 sets × 10 reps
Hinge at hips, slight bend in knees, lower bar along legs. Massive hamstring and glute builder. Feel the stretch before driving hips forward.
▶ YouTube Rest 90 sec
Start
Finish
Goblet Squat
3 sets × 12 reps
Hold a dumbbell or kettlebell at chest. Great for quad development and keeping an upright torso. Easier on lower back than barbell.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
Dumbbell Reverse Lunge
3 sets × 10 reps each leg
Step back, knee hovers above floor. More knee-friendly than forward lunge. Works quads, glutes, and balance.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
Dumbbell Step-Up
3 sets × 12 reps each leg
Use your bench as the step. Drive through the heel of the elevated foot. Single-leg work fixes imbalances and builds glute separation.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
Glute Kickback
3 sets × 15 reps each leg
On all fours, kick one leg back and up. Isolates the glute. Slender guys often have flat glutes — this fixes that and adds shape.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Standing Calf Raise
4 sets × 20 reps
Full range — all the way up, all the way down. Pause at top. Calves need high reps. Use DBs for added load.
▶ YouTube Rest 45 sec
Tip: Leg day is the most important session for a skinny guy trying to add overall mass. Heavy squats and RDLs flood your system with testosterone and growth hormone that benefits every other muscle group.
Friday
Shoulders
All three delt heads for wide, capped shoulders. Rest 60 sec between sets.
Start
Finish
Dumbbell Shoulder Press
4 sets × 10–12 reps
Seated or standing. Press from ear level up. Don't lock elbows — keep constant tension on delts throughout.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
Side Lateral Raise
4 sets × 15 reps
Slight bend in elbows, raise to shoulder height only. This creates width. Slow the negative. Most people go too heavy here.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Alternating Front Raise
3 sets × 12 reps each arm
One arm at a time to shoulder height, palm down. Front delt. Slow and controlled — no swinging.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Seated Rear Delt Fly
3 sets × 15 reps
Light weight, feel the squeeze behind the shoulder. Rear delts make shoulders look complete from every angle.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Arnold Press
3 sets × 10 reps
Rotate palms-facing-you at bottom to palms-forward at top. Hits all three delt heads in one movement. Great finisher.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Tip: Wide shoulders on a lean frame are the fastest visual upgrade you can make. Lateral raises are the key — drop to 10–12 lbs and slow down the negative. Width comes from control, not load.
Saturday
Abs + Full Body
Core definition + heavy deadlift. End the week strong. Sunday is full rest.
Core / Abs
Start
Finish
Ab Roller Rollout
3 sets × 8–12 reps
Go as far as possible keeping lower back flat. One of the most effective core exercises. Saw your roller in the gym photos.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Russian Twist with Dumbbell
3 sets × 20 reps (10 each side)
Hold one DB, lean back 45°, rotate side to side. Works obliques — critical for a defined waistline.
▶ YouTube Rest 45 sec
Start
Finish
Flat Bench Leg Raise
3 sets × 15 reps
Lower back pressed flat the whole time. Slow the descent — lower abs fire hardest on the way down. Don't let feet touch.
▶ YouTube Rest 45 sec
Full Body Compounds
Start
Finish
Barbell Deadlift
3 sets × 6–8 reps
Full body king. Heavy enough to feel it everywhere. Drive through the floor, keep bar against legs, lock hips and knees together at top.
▶ YouTube Rest 90 sec
Start
Finish
Kettlebell Swing
3 sets × 20 reps
Hip-hinge power movement. Drives posterior chain and metabolic burn. Great way to close the week with intensity.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Tip: The deadlift here is intentional — it's the best full-body strength movement and ties everything together. Go heavy but controlled. Sunday rest is non-negotiable for your recovery.
6-Day Full Body Program · Muscle + Definition · Built for Home Gym
Progressive overload every 2 weeks · Sunday = full rest
